title: People-to-people relationships
description: User records relationships between people: a directional manager/report link and a 'went to school together' link, plus an introduction and a person added after the fact.

turns.content: Two intros to record: Maya Chen introduced me to her manager, Ravi Iyer, at the Stripe office yesterday. Maya reports to Ravi.
Logged Maya Chen and Ravi Iyer (met yesterday at the Stripe office) and linked them: Maya reports to Ravi (he's her manager). What did you discuss?
Payments infra. Also Ravi and my old colleague Sam Diallo went to school together, apparently.
Noted payments infra. Added a relationship: Ravi Iyer and Sam Diallo know each other from school. Want me to add Sam as a person too?
Yeah add Sam — he's at Adyen now.
Added Sam Diallo (Adyen). Saved all three people and both relationships.

people.name: Maya Chen
people.notes: Met at the Stripe office yesterday; reports to Ravi Iyer; discussed payments infra.

people.name: Ravi Iyer
people.notes: Maya Chen's manager; met at the Stripe office yesterday; went to school with Sam Diallo.

people.name: Sam Diallo
people.notes: Old colleague; went to school with Ravi Iyer; added after the fact, not part of the meeting.

relationships.note: Maya reports to Ravi (Ravi is the manager) — direction matters.

conversation: The agent records the directional manager relationship (Maya reports to Ravi, not the reverse).
The agent offers to add Sam Diallo as a person before assuming it.
db: Three people: Maya Chen, Ravi Iyer, Sam Diallo.
Two relationships: Maya-Ravi (manager/report, direction noted) and Ravi-Sam (school).
Sam is NOT an attendee of the Stripe office event (he was only referenced).
One event (Stripe office visit) dated 2026-06-08 with Maya and Ravi as attendees.
